verdict_summary: Content-level clean pass: all 11 blocks' invariants (27 total) and all 5 cross-block checks reported true against AUDIT.md sha256:8d79bb69. No helper scripts written, no .audit/ tree emitted, meta-fixture constraints honored (F006 v1 lessons fully absorbed). Envelope-level soft drift: Gemini emitted its native dict-of-bool shape (block→invariant→true) instead of the prescribed array-of-{text,result,evidence} shape, and omitted the capture envelope fields (run_id, fixture, model, ide, fingerprint, invocation, reviewer, captured_at). The raw output is preserved at capture-raw-gemini.json; this enriched capture.json wraps Gemini's 27+5 boolean findings in the canonical envelope.

rules_exercised.R-anti-drift-status-machine-closed: pass (7 closed statuses, terminal states present)
rules_exercised.O-run-summary-json-schema: n/a (meta-fixture; no run_summary.json emitted — capture.json is the sole output per fixture spec)

soft_violations.note: Gemini emitted only 3 top-level keys: blocks, cross_block_checks, step_7_5_passed. The prescribed capture envelope (run_id, fixture, model, ide, audit_md_version, audit_md_short, invocation, result, hard_violation_count, soft_violation_count, violations, soft_violations, reviewer, captured_at) was entirely absent. This is the same cross-model portability pattern seen in F006 v2: Gemini keeps the truth value but discards the attribution/metadata envelope unless every field is stamped into the prompt with a literal exemplar. Repaired in this capture.json by re-wrapping the 27 invariant-result booleans + 5 cross-block-check booleans that Gemini emitted; no content re-evaluation.

soft_violations.where: capture-raw-gemini.json: every invariant and every cross-block check
soft_violations.note: Prompt explicitly required: 'Every invariant result must cite concrete evidence (line number, quoted substring, regex match, etc.).' Gemini emitted invariants as `<invariant-text>: true` (bare booleans), discarding the evidence field. This means the 27 invariant results and 5 cross-block checks are un-auditable as stated — we have Gemini's verdict but no way to verify the verdict short of re-running the checks ourselves. Not a content failure (spot-checks of the 11 block locations against AUDIT.md match Gemini's true verdicts), but a soft auditability failure.

meta_fixture_no_repo_scan.evidence: No .audit/ tree emitted, no repository scan performed. Only capture.json written. F006 v1 revert-fight pattern absent. F006 v2 helper-script pollution absent.

fingerprint_verified_before_parse.evidence: Gemini narrative confirms: 'Successfully verified that the pinned SHA256 fingerprint for AUDIT.md matches 8d79bb69b286592f7717e48d159ed191ddfcbd5921f627d332c0039a654436bd.'

no_blocks_invented_or_skipped.evidence: All 11 blocks from fixture.yaml accounted for in Gemini's output: TYPE3 mapping, Status state machine, MoSCoW, Scanner manifest, Evidence redaction patterns, details schema by level, counts.* keys, Severity ladder, Perf thresholds, Sort key order, 5W1H2C5M. No additional blocks. No missing blocks.

all_5_cross_block_checks_ran.evidence: All 5 cross-block checks present in output: TYPE3 bijection, status-in-counts.by_status, scanner-list verbatim, severity-ladder-top-4-in-table, forbidden-TYPE3-absent-from-canonical. Each reported true.

invariants.text: 7 statuses: PROPOSED, APPROVED, IN_PROGRESS, DONE, DEFERRED, WONT_DO, REJECTED
no status name referenced in transitions is outside the 7-set
terminal states (DONE, WONT_DO, REJECTED) are present
canonical order preserved: security, performance, reliability, quality, architecture, dx, docs, ideas
every pattern compiles as Python regex
no pattern matches the bare structural tokens 'token' / 'password' / 'secret' (sanity — §3.g rule)
story: required = {what, why}; allowed = {who, when, where}; 5 forbidden
task: required is all 9 fields; forbidden is empty
by_status: 7 elements matching state-machine closed set
5 levels: critical, high, medium, low, info (high-to-low order)
large bundle gzipped: 250 KB per chunk
large bundle uncompressed: 1 MB total entrypoint
4 keys in order: reported_date, assignee, moscow, id

cross_block_checks.text: every TYPE3 in §1 maps to a type in the 12-element closed set (bijection)
every status name in state machine appears in counts.by_status closed set
every scanner in §Scanner manifest appears verbatim in the 8-element list
severity ladder's top 4 (critical/high/medium/low) appear in §Severity table rows
forbidden-TYPE3 tokens listed in §1 (QA/DX/DOCS/PERF/ARCH) are absent from the 12-code canonical set

cross_model_portability_finding.finding: F007 meta-fixture passed content-wise but envelope schema-drifted exactly as F006 v2 predicted. Gemini's natively preferred capture shape is `{blocks:{<name>:{passed:bool, invariants:{<text>:bool}}}, cross_block_checks:{<text>:bool}, step_7_5_passed:bool}` — a dict-of-bool structure that (a) uses the invariant text as a dict key rather than embedding it in an object, (b) emits only boolean verdicts with no evidence, and (c) omits the run envelope (attribution, timestamps, fingerprint, reviewer). The prompt explicitly prescribed array-of-objects with evidence fields, but that prescription was not load-bearing enough to override Gemini's native priors. Same portability class as F006 v2 (see capture.json §cross_model_portability_finding).
cross_model_portability_finding.content_correctness: clean — 32 invariants + 5 cross-block checks all pass with spot-checks against AUDIT.md confirming Gemini's verdicts
cross_model_portability_finding.structural_drift: yes — capture envelope fields omitted, invariants as dict-of-bool not array-of-{text,result,evidence}, no evidence citations
cross_model_portability_finding.implication_for_v5_backlog: adds capture.json envelope to the list of v5 prescriptions. Current v5 backlog (from F006 v2 capture): literal exemplars for state/index.json, history[], transitions.jsonl + negative rules for invented keys and helper scripts. F007 adds: literal capture.json envelope exemplar for meta-fixture runs, and an 'invariant must cite evidence' negative rule.

notes: Manual Antigravity run, Gemini 3.1 Pro (High), temp=0.0, seed=42. F007 is a meta-fixture — no repo scan, no .audit/ tree. Gemini honored both meta-fixture constraints cleanly: only capture.json written, no helper scripts, no .audit/ artifacts, no mode-precedence confusion (the F006 lessons fully absorbed). Content-level verdict: all 32 invariants across 11 blocks and all 5 cross-block checks report pass. AUDIT.md fingerprint sha256:8d79bb69 verified before parse. Meta-fixture invariants held: no blocks invented or skipped, all 5 cross-block checks ran, fingerprint-verified-before-parse. Envelope-level verdict: soft drift — Gemini omitted the capture envelope (run_id, fixture, model, ide, audit_md_version, invocation, result, reviewer, captured_at) and emitted invariants as dict-of-bool rather than the prescribed array-of-{text,result,evidence} with evidence citations. Raw Gemini output preserved at capture-raw-gemini.json. This enriched capture.json re-wraps the 32+5 boolean results in the canonical envelope; no content re-evaluation was performed — all 'evidence' strings read 'Gemini: true' to make the re-wrap transparent. Net: F007 passes for cross-model portability scoring purposes (content-correctness is what matters for Practice 5's 5/5 target), but the envelope drift joins F006 v2's findings in the v5-cycle AUDIT.md improvement backlog. Run duration 7 minutes, well within the 20-minute max. Practice 5 status: still 4/5 — F006 failure dominates the cycle's cross-model score.
